mounts.comment: Mounted BEFORE auth middleware (errors can occur pre-login)
Webhooks MUST be mounted BEFORE JSON parsing for Stripe signature verification
Stripe Connect webhooks - requires raw body
Faire webhook - requires raw body for signature verification
Admin orchestrator — mounts all admin sub-routers in strict order (specific sub-paths before generic root mounts)
Universal tenant routes (Phase 3: Universal Identifier System)
Public tenant capabilities (resolve, capabilities, effective-capabilities summary) per AUTH_SCOPE_ISOLATION_SPEC.md
Public shop directory, trending, categories, featured products, shop details
Directory orchestrator — mounts all sub-routers in strict static-first order
Storefront routes (materialized view for instant category filtering)
GBP routes (Google Business Profile category search)
Tenant orchestrator — mounts all tenant sub-routers in strict order (sub-resources before /:id)
Tenant inventory transfers (auth temporarily removed for testing)
Feed validation - must be BEFORE tenantCategoriesRoutes to avoid /:tenantId/categories/:id matching /coverage
Auth RBAC endpoints (role-groups, user-groups, permissions, user-access, role-protected routes)
Auth0 MFA routes (custom UI + Auth0 backend)
Security telemetry (no auth required) - mounted first
Google Business Profile OAuth callback at /auth/google/business (matches GOOGLE_BUSINESS_REDIRECT_URI)
OAuth routes (PayPal & Square payment gateway OAuth)
Store reviews also at /api for helpful votes endpoint
Inline tenant CRUD, status, coordinates, subdomains, business hours
Inline tenant profile, GBP category, public tenant endpoints, categories, diagnostics
Inline items/complete, item update, category assignment, needs-enrichment
Inline taxonomy admin status and sync endpoints
Features showcase config, PATCH tenant profile, logo/banner upload
Items list, stats, get, create, update, delete, restore, purge, photo upload
Global audit logger middleware (logs all write operations)
Tenant tier routes (authenticated tenant routes only)
Digital downloads (no global auth - uses access tokens)
Public business hours endpoints (avoids blanket auth on /api/tenant)
Global catalog routes (public access for browsing)
CRM tenant routes (tenant-scoped, requires tenant access)
CRM customer routes (customer-scoped, requires customer JWT auth)
Branding routes (public GET for storefront, auth PUT for merchant)
Security routes (skips /telemetry which has its own mounting)
Tenant flags at /admin (platform admin or store owners)
Scan metrics routes (mounted at root, routes define their own paths)
Brand partner self-service claim submission and lookup
